stories: Good morning Sean, it's Wednesday, the twenty-seventh of May, 2026
Fun fact: eighty-nine years ago today, the Golden Gate Bridge opened to pedestrians for the very first time
Four years and twenty-five million dollars to build
One of the great engineering achievements of the twentieth century
Your calendar is clear today, so it's all yours
On Singapore — a disturbing incident in Choa Chu Kang last night
A woman in her twenties was allegedly stabbed to death at an HDB lift lobby by a man who was reportedly her former partner
He then jumped from the eighteenth floor, landing on a rubbish container

On Singapore, some solid economic news today
The government revised first-quarter GDP growth sharply higher Ã¢ÂÂ up to six percent, from the four-point-six in the advance estimate
Inflation also came in below expectations: headline at one-point-eight percent for April, versus the two percent forecast, and core at one-point-four
The Ministry of Trade and Industry projects full-year growth between two and four percent, though they flagged energy disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz as a continuing risk
